title: NICE Guidelines: GAD Recognition and Diagnosis
content: Consider a diagnosis of GAD in people presenting with anxiety or significant worry, and in people who attend primary care frequently with a chronic physical health problem, or who do not have a physical health problem but attend frequently with reassurance-seeking about somatic symptoms. If GAD is suspected, ask about the person's ability to control their worry and how long they have been feeling like this. Focus on core symptoms: excessive anxiety and worry about a number of events or activities, occurring more days than not for at least 6 months, accompanied by at least three of: restlessness or feeling keyed up or on edge, being easily fatigued, difficulty concentrating or mind going blank, irritability, muscle tension, sleep disturbance.
